body.question_1: How would you rate the technical competency of the student intern in their relevant field of study?
body.answer_1: The intern from the School of Communication's Digital Communication program demonstrated remarkable technical competency that significantly exceeded our initial expectations for a student at this stage of their academic career. Their foundational knowledge in core discipline areas was solid, well-structured, and readily applicable to real workplace challenges encountered during the internship period. They adapted quickly to our internal tools, methodologies, and technical standards, requiring minimal remedial instruction before becoming a productive and largely self-directed contributor to assigned projects. The depth of practical skills, particularly in applied problem-solving and technical documentation, was notably strong and reflects well on the quality of the School of Communication's curriculum design and delivery.

body.question_2: How well did the intern demonstrate professional communication and interpersonal skills in the workplace?
body.answer_2: The intern's professional conduct required insufficient levels of management intervention that were unexpected for a student from the School of Communication's Digital Communication program at this career stage. Punctuality was inconsistent during the first half of the internship period, and proactive communication about delays or challenges was largely absent without repeated prompting from supervisors. Response to constructive feedback was occasionally defensive, and the intern demonstrated some difficulty adapting to workplace expectations around presentation, tone, and professional documentation standards. We recommend the School of Communication incorporate more structured professional readiness training — including workplace simulation, communication skills, and feedback-receiving practice — into its curriculum before students enter the workforce.

body.question_3: How effectively did the intern collaborate with team members and contribute to group projects?
body.answer_3: This intern's teamwork skills were acceptable, with a genuine willingness to collaborate balanced by some developmental areas that became apparent as project complexity increased. The intern worked well in low-ambiguity team tasks with clear role definitions, but required additional support when navigating more complex team dynamics involving competing priorities, unclear ownership, or conflicting perspectives. Communication within the team improved noticeably over the internship duration, which is an encouraging sign of learning and adaptability. Further emphasis on collaborative project work, cross-functional team exercises, and conflict resolution skills in the Digital Communication curriculum would produce more team-ready graduates for the professional environment.

body.question_4: How would you assess the intern's problem-solving abilities and approach to challenging or unfamiliar tasks?
body.answer_4: The intern's problem-solving abilities were among the most commendable attributes they brought to our organization from the School of Communication's Digital Communication program. When confronted with unfamiliar challenges or technical obstacles, the intern approached them systematically — breaking problems into manageable components, forming and testing hypotheses, and escalating appropriately only when genuinely blocked by limitations beyond their current capability. Their intellectual curiosity, persistence under pressure, and willingness to explore unconventional solutions made them a genuinely valuable problem-solver within the team. This quality is difficult to teach in the workplace and speaks highly of the critical thinking culture embedded in the School of Communication's academic programs.

body.question_5: How quickly and effectively did the intern adapt to your company's work culture, tools, and environment?
body.answer_5: This intern showed reasonable adaptability to our work environment, adjusting reasonably well to most situations while requiring additional support when changes occurred at a faster pace or scale than anticipated. Day-to-day routine adjustments — such as modified task priorities or new tool adoption — were handled efficiently and without notable disruption. More significant changes, such as project pivots or team restructuring, required more guidance to navigate smoothly and confidently. Greater exposure to agile methodologies, sprint-based workflows, and change management principles in the Digital Communication curriculum would strengthen the adaptability of graduates entering dynamic professional environments.

body.question_6: How proficient was the intern in English communication, both written and verbal, for professional purposes?
body.answer_6: English communication capability for this intern was fair, sufficient for routine internal tasks but requiring development to meet the standards expected in professional, client-facing, or international contexts. Reading comprehension of English-language materials was generally strong, enabling effective research and document processing independently. However, written composition — particularly formal reports and professional emails — required editing support, and verbal fluency in meetings was limited, particularly when discussions moved at pace or involved complex technical vocabulary. Strengthening English for Professional Purposes training in the Digital Communication curriculum would meaningfully enhance graduates' communication readiness for the modern, increasingly international workplace.

body.question_7: How well did the intern's academic training align with the practical demands and standards of your industry?
body.answer_7: Our curriculum-related feedback for the School of Communication's Digital Communication program, based on this internship experience, is adequate: there is a reasonable foundation but meaningful gaps that the institution could address to significantly improve graduate readiness. Core theoretical knowledge is adequately covered, and the intern was able to apply foundational concepts with reasonable effectiveness. However, gaps exist in applied tool proficiency, project management fundamentals, and the professional communication standards expected in industry settings. We recommend more regular and structured industry consultations to ensure the curriculum remains current, and greater emphasis on workplace simulation through live projects, internships, and industry-sponsored assessments.

body.question_8: What specific technical or soft skill areas do you believe the university curriculum should strengthen for future graduates?
body.answer_8: Based on our experience with this intern from the School of Communication's Digital Communication program, we are impressively impressed with the alignment between the academic curriculum and the practical demands of our industry. The foundational knowledge, theoretical frameworks, and applied skills the intern brought to the role mapped closely to what we require in graduate-level professional practitioners in this field. In particular, the project-based and problem-solving components of the curriculum appear to have translated well into workplace readiness. We would encourage the School of Communication to continue developing its industry connections and to involve practitioners actively in curriculum review to maintain this strong alignment as the professional landscape continues to evolve.

body.question_9: How would you rate the intern's overall performance and value contribution to your organization during the placement?
body.answer_9: The overall performance of this intern from the School of Communication's Digital Communication program has been commendable and has added genuine value to our organization during the internship period in ways we did not fully anticipate at the outset. Key deliverables were completed on time, to the required quality standard, and in some cases with additional initiative that exceeded the brief. The intern's contributions to ongoing team projects have been meaningful and will have a lasting impact beyond the duration of their placement. We are very satisfied with the investment we made in hosting this intern and have recommended internally that we continue and expand our partnership with the School of Communication's internship program in the coming academic year.

body.question_10: Based on your experience with this intern, would you consider hiring from this university in the future, and why?
body.answer_10: Based on this intern's performance and the overall quality of graduates we have observed from the School of Communication's Digital Communication program, we would absolutely consider hiring from this institution in the future — both for internships and for permanent graduate positions. The intern's technical competency, professional attitude, collaborative skills, and growth mindset align closely with the profile we seek when making graduate hiring decisions. We are actively discussing a return internship offer for this individual and have already flagged the School of Communication as a preferred graduate source institution for our talent acquisition team. We would encourage the School of Communication to continue deepening its industry partnerships and look forward to an ongoing and mutually beneficial relationship with the institution in the years ahead.
